heading: Capacity development
label: 300g–9 Capacity development

children.heading: State authority for new systems
children.label: a State authority for new systems
children.text: A State shall receive only 80 percent of the allotment that the State is otherwise entitled to receive under (relating to State loan funds) unless the State has obtained the legal authority or other means to ensure that all new community water systems and new nontransient, noncommunity water systems commencing operation after , demonstrate technical, managerial, and financial capacity with respect to each national primary drinking water regulation in effect, or likely to be in effect, on the date of commencement of operations.section 300j–12 of this titleOctober 1, 1999

children.heading: List
children.label: 1 List
children.text: Beginning not later than 1 year after , each State shall prepare, periodically update, and submit to the Administrator a list of community water systems and nontransient, noncommunity water systems that have a history of significant noncompliance with this subchapter (as defined in guidelines issued prior to , or any revisions of the guidelines that have been made in consultation with the States) and, to the extent practicable, the reasons for noncompliance.August 6, 1996August 6, 1996

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 5 years after , and as part of the capacity development strategy of the State, each State shall report to the Administrator on the success of enforcement mechanisms and initial capacity development efforts in assisting the public water systems listed under paragraph (1) to improve technical, managerial, and financial capacity.August 6, 1996

children.heading: Withholding
children.label: 3 Withholding
children.text: The list and report under this subsection shall be considered part of the capacity development strategy of the State required under subsection (c) of this section for purposes of the withholding requirements of (relating to State loan funds).section 300j–12(a)(1)(G)(i) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: 90 percent in fiscal year 2001;

children.label: B
children.text: 85 percent in fiscal year 2002; and

children.label: C
children.text: 80 percent in each subsequent fiscal year,

children.label: A
children.text: the methods or criteria that the State will use to identify and prioritize the public water systems most in need of improving technical, managerial, and financial capacity;

children.label: B
children.text: a description of the institutional, regulatory, financial, tax, or legal factors at the Federal, State, or local level that encourage or impair capacity development;

children.label: i
children.text: assist public water systems in complying with national primary drinking water regulations;

children.label: ii
children.text: encourage the development of partnerships between public water systems to enhance the technical, managerial, and financial capacity of the systems; and

children.label: iii
children.text: assist public water systems in the training and certification of operators;

children.label: D
children.text: a description of how the State will establish a baseline and measure improvements in capacity with respect to national primary drinking water regulations and State drinking water law;

children.label: E
children.text: an identification of the persons that have an interest in and are involved in the development and implementation of the capacity development strategy (including all appropriate agencies of Federal, State, and local governments, private and nonprofit public water systems, and public water system customers); and

children.label: i
children.text: encourage development by public water systems of asset management plans that include best practices for asset management; and

children.label: ii
children.text: assist, including through the provision of technical assistance, public water systems in training operators or other relevant and appropriate persons in implementing such asset management plans.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 3 Report
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the date on which a State first adopts a capacity development strategy under this subsection, and every 3 years thereafter, the head of the State agency that has primary responsibility to carry out this subchapter in the State shall submit to the Governor a report that shall also be available to the public on the efficacy of the strategy and progress made toward improving the technical, managerial, and financial capacity of public water systems in the State, including efforts of the State to encourage development by public water systems of asset management plans and to assist public water systems in training relevant and appropriate persons in implementing such asset management plans.

children.heading: Review
children.label: 4 Review
children.text: The decisions of the State under this section regarding any particular public water system are not subject to review by the Administrator and may not serve as the basis for withholding funds under .section 300j–12 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator shall support the States in developing capacity development strategies.

children.label: i
children.text: conduct a review of State capacity development efforts in existence on , and publish information to assist States and public water systems in capacity development efforts; andAugust 6, 1996

children.label: ii
children.text: initiate a partnership with States, public water systems, and the public to develop information for States on recommended operator certification requirements.

children.heading: Publication of information
children.label: B Publication of information
children.text: The Administrator shall publish the information developed through the partnership under subparagraph (A)(ii) not later than 18 months after .August 6, 1996

children.heading: Promulgation of drinking water regulations
children.label: 3 Promulgation of drinking water regulations
children.text: In promulgating a national primary drinking water regulation, the Administrator shall include an analysis of the likely effect of compliance with the regulation on the technical, financial, and managerial capacity of public water systems.

children.heading: Guidance for new systems
children.label: 4 Guidance for new systems
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Administrator shall publish guidance developed in consultation with the States describing legal authorities and other means to ensure that all new community water systems and new nontransient, noncommunity water systems demonstrate technical, managerial, and financial capacity with respect to national primary drinking water regulations.August 6, 1996

children.heading: Information on asset management practices
children.label: 5 Information on asset management practices
children.text: Not later than 5 years after , and not less often than every 5 years thereafter, the Administrator shall review and, if appropriate, update educational materials, including handbooks, training materials, and technical information, made available by the Administrator to owners, managers, and operators of public water systems, local officials, technical assistance providers (including nonprofit water associations), and State personnel concerning best practices for asset management strategies that may be used by public water systems.October 23, 2018

children.heading: Variances and exemptions
children.label: e Variances and exemptions
children.text: Based on information obtained under subsection (c)(3), the Administrator shall, as appropriate, modify regulations concerning variances and exemptions for small public water systems to ensure flexibility in the use of the variances and exemptions. Nothing in this subsection shall be interpreted, construed, or applied to affect or alter the requirements of section 300g–4 or 300g–5 of this title.

children.heading: Small public water systems technology assistance centers
children.label: f Small public water systems technology assistance centers

children.heading: Grant program
children.label: 1 Grant program
children.text: The Administrator is authorized to make grants to institutions of higher learning to establish and operate small public water system technology assistance centers in the United States.

children.heading: Responsibilities of the centers
children.label: 2 Responsibilities of the centers
children.text: The responsibilities of the small public water system technology assistance centers established under this subsection shall include the conduct of training and technical assistance relating to the information, performance, and technical needs of small public water systems or public water systems that serve Indian Tribes.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 3 Applications
children.text: Any institution of higher learning interested in receiving a grant under this subsection shall submit to the Administrator an application in such form and containing such information as the Administrator may require by regulation.

children.label: A
children.text: The small public water system technology assistance center shall be located in a State that is representative of the needs of the region in which the State is located for addressing the drinking water needs of small and rural communities or Indian Tribes.

children.label: B
children.text: The grant recipient shall be located in a region that has experienced problems, or may reasonably be foreseen to experience problems, with small and rural public water systems.

children.label: C
children.text: The grant recipient shall have access to expertise in small public water system technology management.

children.label: D
children.text: The grant recipient shall have the capability to disseminate the results of small public water system technology and training programs.

children.label: E
children.text: The projects that the grant recipient proposes to carry out under the grant are necessary and appropriate.

children.label: F
children.text: The grant recipient has regional support beyond the host institution.

children.heading: Consortia of States
children.label: 5 Consortia of States
children.text: At least 2 of the grants under this subsection shall be made to consortia of States with low population densities.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 6 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to make grants under this subsection $2,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 1997 through 1999, and $5,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2000 through 2003.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator shall provide initial funding for one or more university-based environmental finance centers for activities that provide technical assistance to State and local officials in developing the capacity of public water systems. Any such funds shall be used only for activities that are directly related to this subchapter.

children.heading: National capacity development clearinghouse
children.label: 2 National capacity development clearinghouse
children.text: The Administrator shall establish a national public water system capacity development clearinghouse to receive and disseminate information with respect to developing, improving, and maintaining financial and managerial capacity at public water systems. The Administrator shall ensure that the clearinghouse does not duplicate other federally supported clearinghouse activities.

children.heading: Capacity development techniques
children.label: 3 Capacity development techniques
children.text: The Administrator may request an environmental finance center funded under paragraph (1) to develop and test managerial, financial, and institutional techniques for capacity development. The techniques may include capacity assessment methodologies, manual and computer based public water system rate models and capital planning models, public water system consolidation procedures, and regionalization models.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $1,500,000 for each of the fiscal years 1997 through 2003.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 5 Limitation
children.text: No portion of any funds made available under this subsection may be used for lobbying expenses.
